items.title: Parents of children in foster care say 'black eyes' and bite marks were ignored

children.content: The parents of two children in Northern Territory foster care have spoken out about their "heartbreaking" experience with the system, saying concerns their daughter was being abused by her foster family were brushed off by the department.

gallery.altText: A woman in a sleeveless, long dress, long brown hair in kitchen, blurred background, with brown teddy bear focus in foreground.

thumbnail.altText: A woman in a sleeveless, long dress, long brown hair in kitchen, blurred background, with brown teddy bear focus in foreground.

items.title: Former NT political staffer claims unfair dismissal, airs bullying allegations

children.content: A former staffer for Northern Territory government backbencher Clinton Howe is claiming she was sacked for refusing to falsify constituent information in a database, and was bullied and micromanaged by the politician's full-time electorate officer.

image.altText: A large photo of a smiling politician fills the front window of an office building, the front door reads: Clinton Howe

items.title: Nampijinpa Price: 'Extremist ideology' destroying Australian culture

children.content: Jacinta Nampijinpa Price has denied that she agreed with a podcaster saying Australia should stop 'flooding' Australia with migrants from Asia, Africa and the Middle East.

image.altText: Three people walking - men either side of one Aboriginal woman in focus. She has a soft expression, dark blue top and blazer on.

items.title: Defence minister 'disappointed' by lawsuit over Darwin port ownership

children.content: Chinese-owned company Landbridge launched legal action earlier this year following a pledge by the Prime Minister to return the port to Australian hands. Defence Minister Richard Marles says the government will fight the suit and remained committed to finding a domestic buyer.

image.altText: White man standing in front of podium, light pink shirt, navy blue pants, mid-speech, man behind him in white shirt.

items.title: Arafura Rare Earths mine gets green light to challenge China's dominance

children.content: Arafura Rare Earths has announced its flagship project north of Alice Springs will go ahead, decades after it was first flagged as a prospect.

image.altText: a drone shot of a mining camp in Central Australia.

items.title: NT Health authorities MIA as diphtheria spreads across the outback

children.content: As diphtheria spreads across four different Australian jurisdictions in the largest outbreak since records began, public health messaging in the NT — the region most impacted by the serious disease — has been hard to come by.

articles.title: Health officials warn cases of diphtheria could rise

children.content: Cases of diphtheria could continue to rise as the number of people affected reaches 220 across the country — the biggest outbreak of the disease since national records began in 1991.

image.altText: A doctor with his arms folded stands in front of a window

articles.title: Outbreak of highly contagious infection grows to more than 220 cases

children.content: Federal and state governments have mobilised to address the largest outbreak of diphtheria in recent memory, with the Commonwealth preparing a support package for a disease once considered almost eradicated.

image.altText: A young child gets there nose blown.

articles.title: Australia's worst diphtheria outbreak 'for decades' spreads to WA, Qld and SA

children.content: An ongoing diphtheria outbreak in the Northern Territory has now spread across its borders to Western Australia, Queensland and South Australia, with Health Minister Mark Butler describing it as the nation's worst "for decades".

image.altText: Mark Butler wearing a dark suit.

gallery.altText: A woman holding up her t-shirt sleeve, revealing a small band-aid over the spot where she was vaccinated.

thumbnail.altText: A woman holding up her t-shirt sleeve, revealing a small band-aid over the spot where she was vaccinated.

items.title: Peak body slams ministers' failure to attend Aboriginal housing talks

children.content: The Northern Territory's peak body for Aboriginal housing says it's "disgraceful" ministers from both levels of government have failed to attend a conference on the housing crisis.

image.altText: A woman with curly brown hair and in a brown collared shirt looks at the camera

items.title: WHO-linked report warns world more at risk of pandemic now than before COVID

children.content: As health leaders face deadly outbreaks of Ebola and hantavirus, a major report finds the world's response system remains dangerously underprepared.

image.altText: Crowd of people wearing face masks walking across a scramble crossing in Brisbane CBD.

items.title: What we know about Iran's new system of transit in the Strait of Hormuz

children.content: Iran is asserting unprecedented control over the strategically critical waterway, with ships reportedly facing screening processes, transit approvals and potential Bitcoin-backed insurance requirements.

image.altText: A drone view shows the Malta-flagged tanker Agios Fanourios I arriving in Iraq's territorial waters off Basra.

items.title: Snowy bosses paid $1.2m in bonuses as hydro project costs spiral

children.content: The renewables project's chief executive, Dennis Barnes, received $323,000 in bonus pay on top of his $1.69 million salary last financial year, while four other senior executives received about $919,000 in "variable pay".

image.altText: A man wearing a construction outfit, speaking at a lectern.

items.title: Cuba on the brink: Residents describe life 'trapped between two psychopaths'

children.content: As power and petrol supplies run out, Cubans tell the ABC about their daily lives. "You can no longer find streets packed with travellers, music and dance," one says. "Only Cuban nationals, still attempting to take some bread home."

articles.title: Cuba accepts offer of $140m in American aid, Rubio says

children.content: Tensions between the longtime Cold War rivals have hit a new low in recent days after the US indicted the country's 94-year-old former president Raúl Castro.

image.altText: Marco Rubio speaking to reporters outside.

items.title: One Nation surfs wave as Labor defends budget

children.content: Before the budget, Anthony Albanese and Jim Chalmers knew they would be hit by criticism for its broken promises, but they underestimated the extent and ferocity of the backlash.

image.altText: A man in an Akubara wraps his arms around a woman with a microphone

items.title: Chalmers asked to explain how he 'landed on 30 per cent' tax rate

children.content: The Productivity Commission was not asked to model how changes to capital gains tax would impact Australia's economy. Business groups say Treasurer Jim Chalmers must explain how the government concluded its tax changes would ultimately be a boon to productivity.

image.altText: Jim Chalmers speaks to reporters.

items.title: Sydney council slaps dog walkers and gender reveals with new park fees

children.content: A picnic vendor told the ABC they found out about the proposed fees through a group chat with other small businesses, and fears the proposed fee structure could "kill an entire industry".

gallery.altText: A woman sits on a picnic rug with a view of Sydney Harbour behind her.

thumbnail.altText: A woman sits on a picnic rug with a view of Sydney Harbour behind her.

items.title: Early lung damage in kids with CF linked with immune cell changes

children.content: Australian researchers have mapped how lung damage may begin early in life for children with cystic fibrosis, providing new insights that may help reshape future care.

image.altText: A boy does a lung test in a room.

items.title: The hidden codes and Aussie influence in Harry Connick Jr's new symphony

children.content: Harry Connick Jr's new symphony, Elaboratio, is filled with musical tributes to his mother Anita. Orchestra Victoria and Australian conductor Jessica Gethin are bringing those memories to life at Carnegie Hall.

image.altText: A man in a black jacket with an earpiece holds a microphone with a red and purple background

items.title: The Mushroom Tapes, Mad Mabel and Memorial Days triumph at book awards

children.content: In May 2025, Helen Garner, Chloe Hooper and Sarah Krasnostein, three literary true-crime writers, headed to Erin Patterson's trial. The resulting book just won an Australian Book Industry Award.

gallery.altText: Sarah Krasnostein, 48, and Chloe Hooper, 52, sit on either side of a table. Helen Garner, 83, stands between them.

items.title: Friday news quiz: A pumped prince, a grinning sculpture and a stowaway frog

children.content: You'll be as happy as Prince William when you take this week's quiz, which goes off like a frog in a
bag of lettuce.

image.altText: Kylie Minogue sings into a microphone while wearing a rainbow-coloured dress.

items.title: Soft plastics industry at breaking point, as waste instead sent overseas

children.content: The soft plastic recycling industry wants more waste to stay here, saying too much material is being sent overseas.

image.altText: A white haired man with glasses, a blue collared shirt and bright yellow vest staring at piles of plastic while holding a piece.

items.title: Dismay, anger over 1870s mansion reduced to rubble

children.content: How did a colonial-era mansion once earmarked to be on the state heritage register get bulldozed?

items.title: Half Australia's farms are in trusts. So what does tax reform mean for farmers?

children.content: Despite winning a carve-out to changes to family trusts announced in the budget, farmers are still worried about what tax reform means for them.

image.altText: people working in a field

items.title: 'I've never been so confused': Raiders slam officials, fall to Dolphins in thriller

children.content: The Raiders huffed and puffed but couldn't overcome the depleted Dolphins, who despite missing six key players through injury and Origin won a fourth-straight NRL game for the first time.

image.altText: RIcky Stuart looks on

items.title: 'Emotional' Hawks kick off Tassie farewell as star Crow injured

children.content: Blake Hardwick emerges the unlikeliest of heroes for Hawthorn as the Hawks record a nine-point win over Adelaide in Tasmania.

image.altText: Blake Hardwick raises both arms in the air in celebration

items.title: UK trade staff told to be 'careful and sometimes strict' in dealing with Andrew

children.content: The historic documents show no security vetting took place at the time.

items.title: Father of flotilla detainee makes serious allegations against Israel during live interview

children.content: Israel's ambassador to Australia Hillel Newman has stopped short of apologising to the Global Sumud Flotilla activists detained in Israel who had been taunted by far-right minister Itamar Ben-Gvir.

articles.title: Israel supported Ben-Gvir's callousness, until it didn't

children.content: Benjamin Netanyahu has condemned a video of his national security minister taunting activists, including Australians, who have been detained. But it's the prime minister who allowed this behaviour to percolate.

image.altText: Ben-Gvir looks furious as he points at prisoners during a visit.

items.title: Air France and Airbus fined for 2009 crash that killed 228

children.content: France's national airline Air France and Airbus have both been found guilty of involuntary manslaughter over a 2009 crash that killed 228 people.

image.altText: An Air France plane taxiing.

items.title: Drivers allege Bruce Highway speed camera used for 'revenue raising'

children.content: The trailer-mounted speed camera has become a regular feature on the Bruce Highway, but these drivers claim they've been "unfairly" targeted in a blatant cash grab.

image.altText: A group of people standing staggered on the steps outside Bundaberg Magistrates Court.

items.title: Millions expected to attend Vivid Sydney as drone shows return

children.content: Dazzling drone shows will return to Sydney's largest art and culture festival for the first time since 2024.

items.title: Berlin approves bid to host Olympics on 100th anniversary of Nazi-run Games

children.content: Berlin's state parliament approves a bid to host the Olympics in 2036, on the 100-year anniversary of the Games hosted by Adolf Hitler and the Nazis.

image.altText: A black-and-white photo of men standing in front of an African American man in a USA tracksuit.

items.title: AI-generated resumes making hiring 'incredibly difficult', recruiters say

children.content: AI-perfected CVs are becoming the norm as more job applicants and employers use artificial intelligence, but recruiters say the technology could be making hiring trickier.

image.altText: A close up of text on a computer screen over a woman's shoulder.

items.title: What is a super El Niño?

children.content: Forecasts are predicting that a "super" El Niño could form later this year.
The severity of these weather patterns tends to depend on ocean temperatures, which we know are rising.
So, there are fears this could be the strongest El Niño ever.
But what exactly is that going to look like for Australia and the world?

image.altText: Image shows a blue background with clouds and white writing over the top that reads: What is a super El Nino?

articles.title: El Niño looms as BOM releases long-range winter forecast

children.content: The Bureau of Meteorology is forecasting winter to be unusually warm across the entire country as an El Niño climate pattern is increasingly likely to take hold.

image.altText: A cloudy day over the water at Williamstown

gallery.altText: Image shows a blue background with clouds and white writing over the top that reads: What is a super El Nino?

thumbnail.altText: Image shows a blue background with clouds and white writing over the top that reads: What is a super El Nino?

items.title: Olympics minister stands aside after electoral commission makes referral to police

children.content: Queensland's Olympics Minister Tim Mander has stood aside from cabinet after he was referred to police by the Australian Electoral Commission.

image.altText: Tim Mander in Queensland parliament.

items.title: Pauline Hanson caught swearing as staffer says 'shut up' to journalist

children.content: Pauline Hanson and her party have again been caught in tense moments with media, with the One Nation leader captured on camera swearing and a staffer telling a journalist to "shut up".

image.altText: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson at a media conference.

items.title: Caravan repairers battling to stay afloat as travellers stay close to home

children.content: Normally at this time of year, RVs are hitting the road to head north and escape winter. But the ongoing fuel crisis is forcing travellers to stay closer to home, causing a serious economic downturn for many of the 2,500 businesses in the RV repair industry.
